content: Just as 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems selectively retain only high-information, curiosity-prioritized experiences to avoid retrieval bloat and improve long-term task performance, general-purpose autonomous agents can be re-framed as inherently memory-constrained active learners that prune low-curiosity interactions at storage time to adapt faster to open-ended environments. This creates a new framing where an agent’s general purpose capability is not limited by raw memory size, but by how well its active memory selection aligns with its intrinsic curiosity drive.
